2026-07-11
Things have gone a bit crazy.
The core functionality should be a work tracking graph, similar to beads but with much more
structure and verifiability. It should allow the kind of historical view of the work that is mentioned in other documents.

Performing work using that graph is a separate issue, and the review flow is part of that.
That is me wanting a better way to interact with agents, and having a tool to process work in the graph with.
This is essentially my own harness, but where work does not happen through a chat interface, but instead only based on some
task store.
This should be a tool that can use, and benefit from, the linka core, but should technically be fully functional with any kind
of store. It just needs to have some abstraction for where to put things like agent interactions, review notes etc. and some way of getting
information on what work to do, and register completion or failures.

Worktrees are a followup concern altogether, and only relevant for concurrent work.
Otherwise branches are fine.
We shold leave this for last, when single-worker/multi-branch workflow works.
